# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  def search
    # Initialize an empty array of products
    @products = []

    # If there are any search parameters in the request, build a dynamic query using where() with string interpolation
    if params[:search].present?
      # Get the search terms from the request parameters
      search_terms = params[:search]

      # Use where() with string interpolation to build a dynamic query for each search term
      search_terms.each do |term|
        @products += Product.where("name LIKE ? OR category LIKE ?", "%#{term}%", "%#{term}%")
      end
    end

    # Sort the products by name and price
    @products = @products.sort_by { |product| [product.name, product.price] }
  end
end
